notes: RDBMS remained dominant database paradigm; Universal Server features absorbed into mainstream RDBMS.
Universal Server concept absorbed into mainstream RDBMS by 2000; PostgreSQL remains canonical open-source implementation.
DataBlade architecture for complex data type extensions; IBM maintained basic DataBlade support post-acquisition.
Aberdeen correctly assessed OODBMSs as niche; market voted for object-relational over pure OODBMS.
ROLAP became mainstream BI architecture; now standard in data warehouse/BI platforms.
UDTs became standard SQL feature in SQL:1999 and beyond; foundational to PostgreSQL extension ecosystem.
Spatial extensions became standard via PostGIS, Oracle Spatial, SQL Server Spatial; foundational to GIS applications.
Full-text search became standard RDBMS feature; also spawned dedicated search engines (Elasticsearch, Solr).
Intranet as database integration challenge; correctly identified as major driver for complex data type needs.
